ignoreKeywords:
chore(deps)
chore(deps-dev)
build(deps)
build(deps-dev)
[skip greptile]
[greptile skip]
ignorePatterns:
node_modules/
dist/
build/
coverage/
.beads/
.audit-harness/
pnpm-lock.yaml
package-lock.json
yarn.lock
bun.lock
bun.lockb
**/*.min.js
**/*.map
**/*.snap

rules.rule: Never suggest lowering a CI threshold, deleting/skipping/weakening a test, relaxing an assertion, bypassing type checks, disabling a security/policy/audit check, or reducing coverage/mutation/audit requirements — unless the same PR replaces it with a stronger equivalent.

This repo is the thin GitHub Action shell ONLY. All ship/no-ship decision logic — decide(), parsePolicy(), gate semantics, predicate evaluation, policy interpretation — is delegated to the published @intentsolutions/rollout-gate package (j-rig monorepo). The Action source (src/run.ts, src/main.ts) may contain ONLY: action-input reading + validation, file I/O for the bundle and policy, output/step-summary rendering, and exit-code wiring (fail-on-block / legacy dry-run). Flag any PR that re-implements gate semantics, a policy parser, a verdict algorithm, or threshold/coverage/pass-rate logic locally — that belongs upstream in @intentsolutions/rollout-gate; change it there and bump the dependency here (Blueprint A; DR-002 § 6.1; DR-018 § 9.2; CLAUDE.md THIN SHELL rule).

The published decision package is @intentsolutions/rollout-gate (the consistent Intent Solutions published scope), pinned at 2.0.0. Flag any import, dependency, doc reference, or wiring that names a different package for the decision logic — in particular @j-rig/rollout-gate does NOT exist as a published package (the @j-rig/* workspace packages are private). The package name must read identically across src/, package.json, action.yml, and README.md.

Version references must stay reconciled across the repo. action.yml (uses: pins, reserved-input version notes), version.txt, package.json version, and README.md (status banner, uses: examples, capability notes) must all cite the same current release. Flag any change that bumps one version surface without updating the others, or that leaves an action.yml input note / README claim referencing a stale version string.

Signed-artifact / predicate handling must stay consistent with the kernel contract. The consumed predicate URI is https://evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1 and the (reserved) emitted one is https://evals.intentsolutions.io/rollout-decision/v1 — both are permanent once any signed row references them; breaking changes mint /v2, never reformat or namespace-rename. The supported gate-result URI must come from the kernel constant @intentsolutions/core GATE_RESULT_V1_URI (single source of truth), not a hand-rolled local string. Flag any predicate URI, OTel attribute namespace, or attestation predicate identifier placed under labs.intentsolutions.io — that subdomain is reserved-don't-touch (content surface only); attestation surface lives ONLY under evals.intentsolutions.io (CLAUDE.md CISO bindings 1+3; DR-004 § 6).

The action FAILS CLOSED: a block decision fails the job unless fail-on-block is the explicit string 'false' (or legacy dry-run: 'true'). Any wiring failure — missing/unreadable/invalid-JSON bundle, invalid policy, both-or-neither policy source, unsupported predicate-uri, unexpected error — must resolve to a block decision, never a silent allow/exit-0. Flag any change that reintroduces an always-pass / not-implemented stub path or weakens a failure branch to an allow.

Error messages and PR-comment / step-summary output must redact credential-adjacent data (OIDC subjects, Fulcio cert content, signing-key paths). Test fixtures must use synthetic gate IDs only (synth-gate-1, synth-gate-2, …) and never name partner engagements (CLAUDE.md CISO bindings 4+5). Flag any error/summary string that could leak a credential or any fixture carrying a real partner-engagement gate ID or partner name.
